entries.back_en: Besides, he is well-liked and the Mikado's confidant.
entries.note: Source truncated; target adds 'confidante do Mikado' not explicit in fragment.

entries.back_en: Oh? Master Haku, how my heart singeth to hear
thee call me "Maro"! Oh, what joy!
But lo! Master Haku. Words about your
situation have reached my ears, in due
entries.note: 'assured' rendered as 'devida' (due); slight shift, though fragment.

entries.back_en: Surely, Lord Osht's request—ah, Master Ukon's!
entries.note: Target pulls 'request' forward and adds 'o pedido'; reordering across lines.

entries.back_en: The mission milord entrusted to you,
entries.note: 'request!' moved to prior line; meaning preserved.

entries.back_en: Speak, speak! Perhaps you are in need of
counsel? Wisdom? Hold not your tongue, for Maro
is here!
Wh-What do you desire of me?
I-In what capacity, tell me?
Why hello!
Wh-What choice do I have, after all?
A friend needs Maroro's aid, and so he
So be it! You may leave everything in Maroro's
Wh-Why do you speak such cruelty?
D-Do you despise me so?
M-Mistress Nekone? I beg you keep not silent
My erudition is to blame? What nonsense is
this? A scholar is a valuable asset,
B-But...
I-I see. Perhaps you could take me as... a
friend? Nothing more?
Verily, a reduced rate of recompense
is no obstacle to service rendered to a friend.
Heavens!
Urgh. That... I...
But what of Lady Nekone, Lady Rulutieh?
A scholar of philosophy, surely, commands
far more a--
Your insinuations wound me, o great sage
Gah! No, I beg you—I did not mean to offend
entries.note: 'meant naught in the way of [offense]' rendered idiomatically.
